chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْهَدْيِ يَعْطَبُ أَوْ يَهْلِكُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَبْلُغَ مَحِلَّهُ وَ الْأَكْلِ مِنْهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 183 – The sacrificial animal gets exhausted or persihes before it reaches its (slaughtering) place, and the eating from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/171)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/171)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْبَدَنَةِ يُهْدِيهَا الرَّجُلُ فَتُكْسَرُ أَوْ تَهْلِكُ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ هَدْياً مَضْمُوناً فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهِ مَكَانَهُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ مَضْمُوناً فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ قُلْتُ أَ وَ يَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from a man who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the camel which the man offers as a sacrifice, so it either breaks (a body part), or it perishes. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If his sacrificial animal was ensured, so upon him would be (another one) in its place; and if it did not happen to be ensured, so there is nothing upon him’. I said, ‘And can he eat from it?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from a man who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who offers a badanah but it suffers damage or is destroyed. He (the Imam) said, ’If it is a binding obligation, he owes its replacement, otherwise, he is not obligated for anything in this matter.’ I then asked, ‘Can he use it for food?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so.’”
